title: Set 03 - CRL, OCSP, Diffie-Hellman, IPsec & Secure Email
subject: PKI
topic: CRL, OCSP, Diffie-Hellman, IPsec & Secure Email

questions.question: Why would a digital certificate be added to a Certificate Revocation List (CRL)?
options: If the public key had become compromised
If the private key had become compromised
questions.description: A certificate is revoked when its associated private key is compromised, lost, or stolen.

questions.question: What does OCSP stand for?
options: Open Certificates for Security and Privacy
questions.description: OCSP allows clients to check certificate revocation status in real time without downloading an entire CRL.

questions.question: Which of these is not an encryption algorithm?
questions.description: Diffie-Hellman is a key exchange algorithm, not an encryption algorithm.

questions.question: In which IPSec mode is data protected from the Transport Layer to the Network Layer?
questions.description: Transport Mode encrypts only the payload while leaving the original IP header intact.

questions.question: Which protocol provides authentication at the IP layer of the OSI model?
questions.description: AH provides data integrity, authentication, and anti-replay protection, but not encryption.

questions.question: SSL protocol provides:
questions.description: Traditional SSL/TLS provides confidentiality, integrity, and historically supported compression.

questions.question: Which security protocol is commonly used for sending and receiving secure email?
questions.description: PGP is designed for secure email encryption, digital signatures, authentication, and compression.

questions.question: Which protocol provides authentication or encryption, or both, for packets at the IP level?
questions.description: ESP provides encryption and can optionally provide authentication, integrity, and anti-replay protection.

questions.question: The combination of key exchange, hash, and encryption algorithms defines a ______ for each SSL session.
questions.description: A cipher suite specifies the key exchange, authentication, encryption, and hash or MAC algorithms used in a TLS session.

questions.question: Kerberos is a ______ protocol.
questions.description: Kerberos is a network authentication protocol that uses tickets and symmetric cryptography.
